title: Ensure no root account access key exists

input_row.title: Ensure no root account access key exists
input_row.description: Access keys provide programmatic access to a given Alibaba Cloud account. It is recommended that all access keys associated with the root account be removed.
input_row.rationale: An Alibaba Cloud account can be viewed as a “root” account. The root account has the highest privilege of an Alibaba Cloud account. Removing access keys associated with the root account limits the opportunity that the account can be compromised.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if the root account has access keys: Using the management console: 1. Logon to Resource Access Management (RAM) console https://ram.console.aliyun.com/overview by using your Alibaba Cloud account (root account). 2. In the left-side navigation pane, click Overview. 3. In the Security Check section, make sure that No AK for Root Account is marked as Finished.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to delete or disable active root access keys: Using the management console 1. Logon to RAM console by using your Alibaba Cloud account (root account). 2. Move the pointer over the account icon in the upper-right corner and click AccessKey. 3. Click Continue to manage AccessKey. 4. On the Security Management page, find the target access keys and perform the following operations: o Click Disable to disable the target access keys temporarily. o Click Delete to delete the target access keys permanently.
input_row.impact: Programs that already use root account access keys may stop working if you disable or delete the access keys without replacing them with other RAM user access keys in your program.
input_row.default_value: By default, no access key is created for the root account.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- Since the control focuses on checking the existence of access keys for the root account, this can be directly queried through Alibaba Cloud APIs or the Aliyun CLI. 
- The audit steps provided can be translated into automated checks using CLI commands or API calls, which satisfy the criteria for an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ram_root_access_key_removal_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Aliyun CLI command: `aliyun ram DescribeAccessKeys --UserName <root_account>` to list the access keys for the root account.
- The expected configuration to validate is that there are **no access keys** listed for the root account.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** No access keys are returned for the root account.
  - **Fail:** One or more access keys are returned for the root account.
- If the command returns any access keys, it indicates non-compliance and requires remediation as outlined in the audit and remediation sections.

This automated audit will help ensure that the Alibaba Cloud environment aligns with the CIS compliance requirements by verifying the absence of root access keys programmatically.
